title: Spicy Crisp Mexican Poblano Tlayudas
description: Spicy, crisp, hangover-cure Mexican dish featuring curry powder, served on a pandan leaf wrap.
instructions: Heat a tandoor or grill to 450°F.
Roast the poblano peppers for 10 minutes on each side, or until charred.
Remove the peppers from the tandoor, let cool, then peel and chop.
Sauté the onion and garlic in olive oil until softened.
Add the curry powder and red pepper flakes to the onion mixture and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the lime juice and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped poblano, cumin, salt, and cooked onion mixture to a bowl and toss to combine.
Place a pandan leaf on a flat surface and spoon a small amount of the poblano mixture onto the center of the leaf.
Fold the leaf over the filling and secure with a toothpick.
Repeat with the remaining filling and leaves.
Grill the tlayudas for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until crispy and golden brown.
Serve with sour cream, cilantro, and Monterey jack cheese.
Cut into wedges and serve immediately.
